commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=26e73938ac4c941b127f80cefda150f347544e08)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `confirmedThreadTs` to Slack send results, fails non-media threaded text/block sends when confirmation is missing or mismatched, preserves threaded media uploads, and adds focused Slack tests.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +214. Total +249 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main falls back from Slack-confirmed `message.thread_ts` to requested `opts.threadTs`, and direct callers still treat the result as success. I did not establish a live Slack reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers combine concrete Slack send-boundary defects, missing real Slack proof, and maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed compatibility change.

target.title: fix(slack): fail unconfirmed threaded deliveries
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Slack threaded outbound sends could report success even when Slack did not confirm delivery into the requested thread. The send layer previously exposed `threadTs` by falling back to the requested value, so callers could not distinguish a real Slack-confirmed thread from an echoed request.

The first repair also applied that strict confirmation check to the media upload path. That was too broad because Slack media uploads legitimately preserve the requested thread through their upload flow rather than returning the same confirmation field used by text sends.

## Why This Change Was Made
This change separates requested thread fallback from Slack-confirmed thread evidence on text sends while keeping threaded media uploads on their existing delivery path. `sendMessageSlack` exposes `confirmedThreadTs` only when Slack returns `message.thread_ts`, and Slack outbound text/block paths fail a requested threaded text delivery unless the confirmed value matches the target thread.

The confirmation guard is shared between the payload/block adapter and `slackPlugin.outbound.sendText`, so plain threaded text sends and threaded payload finalizers now enforce the same delivery proof. Media sends remain exempt from the text-message confirmation guard.

## User Impact
When Slack omits or changes the destination thread for a text or block message, OpenClaw now surfaces a delivery failure instead of silently marking the reply as delivered to the original thread. Successful threaded Slack text/block sends are unchanged.

Threaded Slack file/media uploads keep working and are not rejected just because the text-message confirmation field is absent.

## Evidence
Head commit: `26e73938ac4c941b127f80cefda150f347544e08`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-delivery.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts` passed: 5 files, 86 tests.
- `pnpm oxfmt --check extensions/slack/src/channel.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/slack/src/thread-delivery-confirmation.ts extensions/slack/src/channel.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-delivery.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.ts` passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Regression coverage now includes:

- threaded payload/block sends fail when Slack omits the confirmed thread id;
- threaded `sendText` fails when Slack omits the confirmed thread id;
- successful threaded text/block sends continue to pass when Slack confirms the requested thread;
- threaded media uploads preserve the requested `thread_ts` path and are not rejected by the text-send confirmation guard.

Live Slack proof is still not included from this environment because no redacted Slack workspace credentials are available here.
target.author: lin-hongkuan

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
